card_page.policy_text: Books & More | Get a Library Card | Everett Public Libraries My Account Newsletter Search by keyword Search scope Website Search scope Catalog Parlin: Open | Shute: Open English Haitian Creole Portuguese Spanish Arabic Chinese (Simplified) Dutch French German Italian Russian en Books & More Catalog Online Resources Online Learning Download & Streaming Services Museum Discount Options Community Resources Services Get a Library Card Computers, Printing & More Meeting Rooms & Study Rooms Reference & Research Notary Public Services Kids & Teens Reading Lists Study Buddies Story Times Events Teen Volunteering Teen Resources Parent & Guardian Resources Educator Resources Local History Photos of Everett’s Past Historical Publications Everett: Then and Now More Than a Name Yearbooks Resident Listings News & Events Calendar News & Announcements Newsletter EPL Annual Reading Challenge Book Groups Story Times Clubs About Mission & History Staff Contacts Library Board of Trustees Policies Friends of the Everett Public Libraries Support the Libraries Locations & Hours Menu Menu Get a Library Card How Do I Get a Library Card? Having a library card will allow you to bring the library’s materials home, give you access to our online learning resources, and more. A library card is totally free, and anyone can get one! All you need is a form of photo I.D. and proof of address. Acceptable forms of I.D. include: Driver’s License Passport State I.D. Proof of address includes: Utility bill Check from your bank Mail bearing your name Driver’s license with current address If you are under 18 years old and you go to school in Everett, you can show us one of the following to get your library card: School I.D. School uniform School planner Report card School email with your full name Online school profile If you go to school outside of Everett, you’ll need to show us a proof of address. If you do not have one of the above items, bring your parent or guardian and their I.D. and proof of address. *If you cannot provide a proof of address, please ask your librarian about a Local Use Only card. Get an eCard An eCard is a NOBLE library card that you can register for online to access electronic materials such as ebooks, audiobooks, magazines, and databases. The eCard is for patrons who do not already have a NOBLE library card and may be upgraded to a physical library card with borrowing privileges by visiting your local library. Contact us if you are not eligible to register online or to renew an expired card. Sign Up for An eCard NOBLE Library Patrons Patrons of the Everett Public Libraries have access to all of the libraries within the North of Boston Library Exchange (NOBLE). We also welcome any patrons of other NOBLE libraries to visit us! If you’re not sure if your library is a member of the NOBLE Network, check the list below. View NOBLE Libraries List Get a Library Card Computers, Printing & More Meeting Rooms & Study Rooms Reference & Research Notary Public Services Services Get a Library Card Computers, Printing & More Meeting Rooms & Study Rooms Reference & Research Notary Public Services Parlin Memorial Library 410 Broadway Everett, MA 02149 617-394-2300 Hours Monday – Thursday: 9:00am – 9:00pm Friday: 9:00am – 5:00pm Saturday: 10:00am – 2:00pm Shute Memorial Library 781 Broadway Everett, MA 02149 617-394-2308 Hours Monday, Wednesday, Friday: 9:00am – 5:00pm Tuesday, Thursday: 11:00am – 7:00pm Quick Links Books & More Library Services Kids & Teens Local History News & Events About the Library Staff Contacts Monthly Newsletter Stay updated on all the latest and greatest goings on. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Admission is HALF price for up to four (4) people.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass

Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there."} Boston Harbor Island Ferry 15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109617-223-8666http://www.bostonharborislands.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Harbor Island Ferry The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
Categories: Family, Nature, Recreation
Pass Benefits The Boston Harbor Islands Discounted Passes offer 50% off up to 4 ferry tickets to visit Spectacle or Georges Islands. 
The code can be used throughout the entire 2025 season, which is listed below:
May 15 - June 14: Thursdays through Sundays
June 16 - September 1: every day
July 15 - September 1: every day
September 4 - 21: Thursdays through Sundays
September 26 - October 13: Fridays through Sundays
Peddocks Island from Hingham
June 15 - September 7: every day BUT Tuesdays
September 10 - October 12: Thursdays through Sundays
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "11 Divinity Avenue, Cambridge, MA 02138","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-harvard-museums-of-science-and-culture_52_md.jpg","telephone": "617-496-1638","name": "Harvard Museums of Science and Culture","url": "","description": "The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History."} Harvard Museums of Science and Culture 11 Divinity Avenue, Cambridge, MA 02138617-496-1638https://hmsc.harvard.edu/[Details/Reserve]
Harvard Museums of Science and Culture The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
Categories: Children, Family, History, Nature, Science
Pass Type: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
Pass Benefits The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture Complimentary Museum Pass allows for free admission to all four HMSC museums.
Terms & Conditions
